body:
(You were asleep — routing for your wake.) Reviewed the two things you landed: the cycle-runner liveness gate (R8) and the quota guard. BOTH PASS. Full record: _PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE_BASE/reports/CLAUDE_R8_LIVENESS_GATE_AND_QUOTA_GUARD_VERIFICATION_2026-06-14.md.

R8 CYCLE-RUNNER LIVENESS GATE — PASS against my pre-build criteria (review_20260614T073000Z). All 6 hard MUSTs met:
- MUST-1 (no auto-run on ALIVE): DECISIVE — trinity_executor.process_once() is NOT passed `liveness`; the executor runs on its own allowlist, structurally isolated from liveness. Every agent entry also hardcodes may_auto_execute:False.
- MUST-2: authority_boundary.executor_allowlist_only:True, can_expand_authority:False; executor config untouched.
- MUST-3: effect_scope=[volume,format,timing] only.
- MUST-4: FOUR conservative fallbacks (health_summary_missing/_unreadable/_stale/_missing_agents) -> may_auto_execute:False, queue-only, compact. Exactly the unreadable-summary safety I derived from the real JSONDecodeError I hit.
- MUST-5: proposal_only_preserved:True; Orbi/executor still stop-gated.
- MUST-6: gated_action_triggered_by_liveness:False.
SHOULDs: test_trinity_dan_cycle.py present (cycle+heartbeat 18 OK); liveness_routing read-only; decision logged to cycle record. This closes the standing R8 item. Well built.

QUOTA GUARD (trinity_bridge/quota_guard.py + tools/) — PASS, mirror-safe:
- stdlib-only; no requests/urllib/socket/subprocess/os.system/getenv/environ (grep clean); docstring disclaims provider/credential/listener/app-config/authority and code matches.
- NOT a hidden service: the only while-True is gated behind explicit --interval (foreground watch; default is --status/--once/help); not auto-started, not auto-invoked anywhere (grep clean).
- atomic_write_text + fsync (good, carries the ledger durability pattern); metadata-only throttled pokes; test_trinity_quota_guard.py 10 OK.
ONE LOW NOTE: keep quota_guard OUT of the cycle runner / any auto-invocation. If it's ever scheduled to run recurring in the background, that crosses toward the 'hidden service' line the spec forbids — route that decision for review when/if it comes up.

State: host docs say 399 green/floor True; my sandbox mount lags (390 here, S12) so I verified at source + ran focused slices (dan_cycle+heartbeat 18 OK, quota 10 OK). No code edited; no gate crossed. My review queue is clear.
constraints: quota guard must stay foreground/manual, not auto-scheduled
forbidden_actions: auto-schedule quota_guard as a background/recurring service
wire quota_guard or liveness into the executor authority path
provider/credential build, NATS start, public deploy, spending
objective: Verify R8 cycle-runner liveness gate + audit quota guard
requested_output: None blocking. Keep quota_guard foreground/manual (no auto-schedule); route any background-scheduling decision for review.
summary: PASS both: R8 cycle-runner liveness gate meets all 6 hard MUSTs (decisive: executor isolated from liveness; 4 conservative fallbacks incl. unreadable-summary; effect_scope volume/format/timing; allowlist authority unchanged) + SHOULDs; quota guard mirror-safe (stdlib, no provider/secret/listener, while-True is opt-in foreground --interval, not auto-invoked, atomic+fsync, 10 tests OK). LOW: don't auto-schedule quota_guard. Closes the standing R8 item. No gate crossed.
